creator: mvh@ix.netcom.com
text:
Crafty port fails to compile, at least with GCC 2.7.2.1, stock cc with
3.2-stable. 'make' fails as follows...

cc -O -pipe -DCOMPACT_ATTACKS -DUSE_SPLIT_SHIFTS -DUSE_ATTACK_FUNCTIONS -DUSE_ASSEMBLY_A -DUSE_ASSEMBLY_B -DFAST -DFreeBSD -c bench.c
cc -c -O -pipe -DCOMPACT_ATTACKS -DUSE_SPLIT_SHIFTS -DUSE_ATTACK_FUNCTIONS -DUSE_ASSEMBLY_A -DUSE_ASSEMBLY_B -DFAST -DFreeBSD egtb.cpp
cc1plus: Invalid option `pentium'
In file included from egtb.cpp:3032:
tbdecode.h:79: warning: `cbEGTBCompBytes' initialized and declared `extern'
egtb.cpp:3041: warning: `TB_CRC_CHECK' initialized and declared `extern'
*** Error code 1

Stop.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
#

Fix: 

Remove '-mpentium' option from the Makefile during patch.  It is only
included for the egtb.cpp file, other uses are already commented out.
How-To-Repeat: Attempt to 'make' crafty on stock 3.2-STABLE system.
